sampleRecalls.title: Lidl US Recalls Silvercrest Bread Makers Due to Electric Shock Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Lidl's Silvercrest bread maker. It is a rectangular shaped white and silver appliance. The bread maker has an electronic display, buttons, and a lid on the top. Affected units have IAN 319996, IAN 304926, or IAN 295433 on a sticker on the bottom of each machine and on the product packaging. "Silve…
hazards: The bread maker's lid can become energized, posing an electric shock hazard.
injuries: There has been one incident of the bread maker becoming energized. No injuries have been reported.

sampleRecalls.title: Trail Crest Recalls Children's Hooded Sweatshirts Due to Strangulation Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Trail Crest brand boys and girls hooded sweatshirts sold in sizes x-small, small and medium. They have a camouflage pattern on shoulders and arms, with drawstrings in the hood and zippers at the neck. They come in birch and camouflage, pink and camouflage, and purple and camouflage. There is a tag…
hazards: The hoodies and jackets have drawstrings through the hood or neck which pose a strangulation hazard to young children.
products: Trail Crest boys and girls hooded sweatshirts and jackets

sampleRecalls.title: Foldable Steel Hammock Stand Sold at Lowe's Stores Recalled
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled hammock stand is black steel, is foldable, and has two wheels. The item number, 226724, is printed on the box in which the hammock stand was sold, and on the instructions that were provided with the hammock stand.
hazards: The welds attaching the arms to the frame of the hammock stand can fail, allowing the hammock to fall. Users can be injured from falling to the ground, strikin…
injuries: There have been three reports of the hammock stands breaking at the welds. Six people have reported injuries from falling, including a head laceration requiring stitches, bruises and neck and back pa…
products: Garden Treasures Foldable Steel Hammock Stand

sampleRecalls.title: CPSC, WaveCrest Laboratories LLC Announce Recall of Electric Bicycles
sampleRecalls.description: The recall involves specific front and rear wheels of TidalForce S-750 High Performance Electric Bicycles. The electric bicycle can travel at speeds of up to 20 miles per hour. The bicycle frame comes in either Candy Apple Red or Silver Satin and has a "TidalForce" sticker on the down tube and an "S-750 High Performan…
hazards: The front and/or rear wheel can collapse, causing the rider to fall and suffer injuries.
products: TidalForce S-750 High Performance Electric Bicycles

sampleRecalls.title: CPSC And Manufacturers Announce Recall Of Wooden Bunk Beds
sampleRecalls.description: May 9, 1995 Release # 95-118 WASHINGTON, D.C. - CPSC and 11 bunk bed manufacturers jointly announced today a recall program for more than 320,000 wooden bunk beds. The bunk beds have openings on the top bunk which may present a potential entrapment hazard to young children. The spaces can be large enough for a child's…
products: Lexington Furniture Industries wooden bunk beds

sampleRecords.event_description: Caught or wedged between objects  nonrunning
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee went to remove the air bar from a roll of plastic sheeting and needed to unjam a shaft that was stuck inside a metal core. He stood on one side of the shaft and core, and went to push/pull the shaft out of the core in order to free the core. The shaft became free all at once, pulling the core toward the employee; his fingers were pinched between the core and the shaft. His ring fingers

sampleRecords.event_description: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was cleaning the cylinder on a press when their left hand was pulled under the cylinder and injured. The employee was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: On January 25, 2025, at 12:30 p.m., an employee slipped on banding that was on the floor, causing them to fall and strike their knee on floor. The employee suffered injuries that required hospitalization.

sampleRecords.nature: Cuts, lacerations, punctures without injury to internal structures
sampleRecords.event_description: Caught, entangled in running powered equipment  normal operation
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was moving milking cows into a milking parlor area. His left hand was in the workings of an automatic "crowd" gate when it indexed. The mechanism caught his hand, crushing and lacerating the hand and fingers.
An employee was cutting down a tree when they were struck by the front tire of an articulating wheeled grapple that was falling. They were hospitalized with a broken lower right leg.
